text:
Hops the Hare was the fastest runner in the forest.
He loved teasing Tilly the Tortoise… "You're sooooo slow! Ha ha ha!"
Tilly looked up calmly.  "Then let's have a race."

Judge Owl raised his flag.
"Ready… set…  GO!"
ZOOM! Hops shot off like a rocket!
Tilly took her first step. One step… two steps… nice and slow.

Hops ran so far ahead, he turned around and laughed.
"Ha! The tortoise is just a tiny little dot!"
He put his hands on his hips and grinned.

Tilly kept walking, one little step at a time.
She whispered to herself…
"It's okay, nice and slow, step by step, here I go."

Hops was too far ahead with nothing to do. So boring!
He chased butterflies. He picked flowers.
"The tortoise is so slow… I can play all day!"

Hops yawned a biiiiig yawn.
"She's so slow… I'll just rest… my eyes… for a second…"
And just like that… Hops was snoring. Zzzzzzz.

Tilly walked and walked… and walked.
She passed right by the snoring hare.
She glanced at him… but didn't stop.
"It's okay, nice and slow, step by step, here I go."

The hill was steep. Tilly was so tired, sweat dripping from her head.
But she didn't stop.
"It's okay… nice and slow… step by step… here I go."

Hops' eyes popped open!
"Oh no! How long was I asleep?!"
He looked around —  the tortoise was GONE!

Hops ran as fast as he could!
But far, far away, near the top of the hill—
Tilly was almost at the finish line!

"It's okay, nice and slow, step by step, here I go."
Tilly crossed the finish line!
"HOORAY! Tilly wins!" everyone cheered!

Hops arrived, panting and puffing.
"You were amazing!" he said. "I'll never be lazy again."
Tilly smiled… "Want to run together next time?"
And from that day on… they became the very best of friends.
